text: Agile methodology is a project management approach often used in software development. Work is divided into short cycles called **sprints**, usually lasting two weeks. Each sprint begins with a planning session where the team selects tasks from a **backlog**, which is a list of all the work to be done. These tasks are described as **user stories** – simple explanations of a feature from the end user’s perspective. Every day, the team holds a brief **standup** meeting to discuss progress and obstacles. After each sprint, the team reviews the work and adapts their plan. This **iterative development** process allows teams to respond quickly to changes and deliver value regularly.
